data.title: Stock index futures rise after sell-off triggered by Walmart's outlook
data.description:
Stock index futures climbed higher on Friday, as markets look to regain some momentum after Thursday's sell-off, which was partly driven by Walmart's disappointing outlook.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) +0.1%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) +0.1%, and Dow futures (INDU) +0.1%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was down 1 basis point to 4.50%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) fell 1 basis point to 4.27%.

U.S. stocks finished with losses Thursday, with the S&P 500 pulling back from a record high, in part as investors balked at Walmart's (WMT) soft financial outlook.

"We saw a moderate risk-off move yesterday, with the S&P 500 falling back from its all-time high," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said.

The main story in the U.S. was a weaker-than-expected forecast from Walmart, which added to nerves about the health of the consumer right now, especially after a soft retail sales print last week, Reid added.

On the economic side, the February PMI composite flash and January existing home sales are due during market hours. The latter is expected to come in at 4.13M.

The February consumer sentiment is forecasted to come in at 67.8.

"Final February U.S. Michigan consumer sentiment data is mainly of use as a source of entertainment, such is the degree of political partisanship," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

However, approval for U.S. President Donald Trump’s economic policies and Trump mega-donor Musk has been falling, and the Michigan sentiment may hint at whether that disapproval is partisan or not, Donovan added.

data.title: Tesla releases software update to address recall of 376K vehicles
data.description:
Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) issued a software update as part of a recall of 376,241 vehicles by the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration.

The update was needed to address a steering issue in certain 2023 Model 3 and Model Y vehicles operating software prior to 2023.38.4.

The printed circuit board for the electronic power steering assist may experience an overstress condition, causing a loss of power steering assist when the vehicle reaches a stop and then accelerates again. A loss of power steering assist can require greater steering effort, especially at low speeds, increasing the risk of a crash, the traffic safety regulator said.

Owners of affected vehicles need to install software release 2023.38.4 or a later software release.

Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) released an over-the-air software update, free of charge, to fix the issue. Owner notification letters are expected to be mailed March 25.

Shares of the EV maker were down 0.24% premarket on Friday.

More on Tesla

data.title: Commodity Roundup: Gold clocks 9th weekly gain amid tariff concerns
data.description:
Gold prices were on course for ninth consecutive weeks of gains as investors seek safety against geopolitical risks and inflation, while Swiss customs data showed that bullion exports from Switzerland rose year-on-year in January, with shipments to the U.S. reaching their highest level in at least 13 years.

According to the Swiss data, gold exports from the country to the U.S. rose to 192.9 tons in January from 64.2 tons in December. This was the highest monthly amount of exports in the customs data going back to 2012.

“This was driven by concerns that the precious metal would be caught up in President Trump’s tariff war,” ANZ Research said.

Bullion (XAUUSD:CUR) eased to about $2,930 an ounce on Friday by 6 am ET, but traded 1.7% higher this week. The safe-haven metal is up over 11% so far this year, ahead of the S&P 500's 4% advance. Much of these gains have been driven by central banks and ETF inflows.

A Bloomberg tally of worldwide ETF holdings swelled to the highest since January 2024, with more than 16 tons added so far this week. That puts them on course for the biggest weekly influx since 2023, in tonnage terms.

According to WGC, global gold ETFs saw $3 billion inflows in January due to European political uncertainties.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:GLD), (NYSEARCA:GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

Elsewhere, the ongoing weakness in the nickel market is starting to take its toll on the Indonesian industry. One of its biggest nickel smelters has slashed production and is close to shutting down. The owner, PT Gunbuster Nickel Industry, is said to be delaying payments to local suppliers and is unable to secure nickel ore.

Nickel futures (LN1:COM) -1.36% to $15,486.88.

data.title: Alibaba's CEO says AGI is 'primary objective' of group's AI strategy
data.description:
Alibaba's (NYSE:BABA) CEO said the pursuit of artificial general intelligence is the company's primary objective when it comes to their AI strategy.

"So when it comes to Alibaba's AI strategy, our first and foremost goal is to pursue AGI. The pursuit of AGI is our primary objective. We aim to continue to develop models that extend the boundaries of intelligence," said Alibaba's CEO Eddie Wu on the company's earnings call on Thursday.

Shares of Alibaba rose about 8% on Thursday after its third quarter results beat estimates. The stock has also climbed about 4% premarket on Friday.

The Chinese e-commerce giant has been developing large language models, or LLMs, to compete with its Western and local rivals. Last month, the company unveiled its Qwen2.5-VL model, which it said fared better than Microsoft (MSFT)-backed OpenAI’s GPT-4o, Amazon (AMZN)-backed Anthropic’s Claude 3.5 Sonnet and Google’s (GOOG) (GOOGL) Gemini 2.0 Flash in math, document analysis, video analysis and question-answering evaluations.

The launch came on the heels of Chinese AI startup DeepSeek (DEEPSEEK) unveiling its AI model R1 and detailed in a paper how LLMs could be built on tight budgets and improved without human supervision.

AGI refers to the hypothetical intelligence of a system which has the ability to understand or learn any intellectual task which a human. It is a type of AI that aims to mimic the cognitive abilities of the human brain.

"In alignment with Alibaba Group's business landscape, we will scale up investments in the following three areas as part of our broader AI strategy over the next three years," said Wu.

The three areas, according to Wu, are — infrastructure for AI and cloud computing; AI foundation models and AI native applications; and transforming the company's existing businesses with AI.

Earlier this month, OpenAI launched a new feature in ChatGPT called "deep research", an agentic capability that conducts multi-step research on the internet for complex tasks. OpenAI noted that deep research marked a significant step toward the company's broader goal of developing AGI.

On Thursday, OpenAI said it had 400 million weekly active users for ChatGPT as of February. The U.S. company's AI technology is not available in China.

data.title: DeepSeek to make some model codes publicly available
data.description:
The China-based artificial intelligence startup DeepSeek said it will be sharing five code repositories publicly, as it looks to progress with “full transparency”

"These humble building blocks in our online service have been documented, deployed and battle-tested in production," DeepSeek said in a post on X.

The post comes a few days after the company was reportedly considering the utilization of outside funding to grow the company.

The launch of the Chinese startup's AI model rattled Silicon Valley and Wall Street, as it outperformed American rivals and was developed at a fraction of the cost, sparking a tech selloff that caused a $1 trillion market wipeout.

However, the firm has been struggling with regulatory scrutiny. Companies and governments around the world have moved to restrict the use of DeepSeek's app due to concerns about potential data leaks.

In early February, two U.S. House lawmakers planned to introduce legislation to ban startup DeepSeek’s (DEEPSEEK) chatbot application from government-issued devices over security concerns the app could turn over user data to China’s government.

data.title: Disney's ESPN, Major League Baseball to part ways after 2025 season
data.description:
Disney's (NYSE:DIS) ESPN and Major League Baseball have "mutually agreed" to end their decades-long broadcast partnership after the 2025 season, with the league saying it was unhappy with the network paring its baseball coverage and pursuing a reduced rights fee.

ESPN has carried MLB games since 1990, and the seven-year deal signed in 2021 - averaging $550M per season - had a March 1 deadline for the league and the network to opt out of the final three years of their contract.

The network said it is still open to pursuing a deal with a reduced rights fee after seeing deals struck with Apple (AAPL) and Roku (ROKU). "Apple pays $85M per season for a Friday night package it has aired since 2022, while Roku's deal for Sunday afternoon games is worth $10M per year," ESPN noted.

But MLB said ESPN has scaled back its baseball coverage and investment "in a way that is not consistent with the sport's appeal or performance on their platform," and its demand to reduce rights fees is "simply unacceptable."

The league added that there is significant interest from traditional media and streaming services for broadcast rights to MLB games. "We will be exploring those opportunities for a new agreement, which would start in the 2026 season following the conclusion of ESPN's agreement at the end of this year."

The 2025 MLB season will begin next month and end in September. To note, the 2024 season drew the highest live attendance in seven years, drawing about 71.3M fans for the games. It was also the most-watched season in five years for ESPN's Sunday Night Baseball.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: MELI, CELH, XYZ, and more
data.description:
Stock futures were little changed on Friday morning as investors digested the rise in initial jobless claims, signaling a modest softening in the labor market. Investors eagerly awaited the consumer sentiment report for further insights into economic conditions and consumer confidence.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • MercadoLibre (NASDAQ:MELI) shares surged 12% after delivering strong Q4 results that exceeded expectations across key metrics, including active buyers, gross merchandise volume (GMV), and payment volume, driving a 37.4% Y/Y increase in total sales. Revenue of $6.1B beat estimates by $120M, fueled by a 33% rise in total payment volume and an 8% increase in GMV. EPS soared to $12.61, nearly quadrupling from the prior year. Brazil, its largest market, saw a 13% GMV increase and a 38% jump in consolidated revenue and financial income.
  • Celsius Holdings (NASDAQ:CELH) jumped 18% after confirming its $1.8B acquisition of rival energy drink brand Alani Nu alongside strong Q4 earnings. The deal, expected to close in Q2, includes $1.65B in price and $150M in tax assets, with $900M in debt financing and $375M in cash on hand. Celsius expects the acquisition to drive topline growth, be cash EPS accretive in year one, and strengthen its position in the global energy drink market, which is projected to grow at a 10% CAGR through 2029. The company also anticipates $50M in run-rate cost synergies over two years.

Biggest stock losers

  • Block (NYSE:XYZ) shares tumbled nearly 7% after missing Q4 earnings estimates and issuing a disappointing outlook for Q1 and FY2025. The company guided Q1 gross profit at $2.32B, below the $2.38B Visible Alpha consensus, and expects adjusted operating income of $430M, up from $402M in Q4 2024. For FY2025, Block forecasted gross profit of $10.22B, trailing the $10.27B consensus but rising from $8.89B in 2024, with expectations for Cash App and Square to improve in the latter half of the year. It reaffirmed its goal to achieve Rule of 40 in 2026 and exit 2025 at or above that threshold on a quarterly basis. CEO Jack Dorsey highlighted plans to integrate its ecosystems, expand sales efforts, and invest in AI tools, while noting increased marketing spending in H2 2024 yielded strong returns.
  • IAMGOLD (NYSE:IAG) shares plunged as much as 9% after falling short of expectations in Q4 results, despite a ~58% Y/Y revenue increase. Looking ahead, the company expects total attributable production in 2025 between 735,000 and 820,000 ounces, up from 2024, driven by Côté Gold’s ramp-up and steady output from Essakane and Westwood. Côté Gold’s first full-year production is projected at 360,000–400,000 ounces (250,000–280,000 ounces attributable). Cash costs are forecasted at $1,200–$1,350 per ounce, slightly higher due to increased costs at Essakane. Capital expenditures are set at $310M, with $290M allocated for sustaining capital.
  • Nu Holdings (NYSE:NU) shares slumped 9% after reporting Q4 earnings and revenue that missed estimates, as purchase volume declined sequentially to $31.1B from $32.6B in Q3 but rose from $23.3B in Q4 2023. The Brazilian digital bank added 5.5M new customers, bringing its total to 99.3M. Monthly average revenue per active customer increased to $11.4 from $10.6 in Q3 and $8.6 a year ago, while the cost to serve per customer remained steady at $0.9, up from $0.8 in Q4 2023.
  • Akamai Technologies (NASDAQ:AKAM) plummeted 9% despite strong Q4 results, as its 2025 guidance fell short of expectations. The company projected adjusted EPS of $6.00–$6.40, missing the $6.82 consensus, and revenue of $4.0B–$4.2B, with a midpoint of $4.1B below the $4.25B consensus. It also forecast an adjusted operating margin of 28%, trailing expectations. For Q1, Akamai expects adjusted EPS of $1.54–$1.59, below the $1.61 consensus, and revenue of $1.0B–$1.02B, missing the $1.04B consensus.
